input_candidates.truth_value: Fracture surface evolution: Zr0 shows small dimples and cleavage faces, while Zr0.01 and Zr0.50 exhibit larger, deeper dimples and increased dimple density, indicating improved ductility.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Room temperature uniaxial tensile tests conducted along rolling direction at strain rate of 10^-3 s^-1 using Instron 5892 with contact extensometer.

input_candidates.truth_value: Formation mechanism of bimodal grain structure in Zr0.50 steel: The combined effects of Zr solute drag and Zener pinning by nanoscale ZrC particles retard recrystallization, leading to partial recrystallization and bimodal grain structure. Micro shear bands from cold-rolling provide nucleation sites for fine grains.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fracture surface morphology: Zr0 steel exhibits small dimples and cleavage faces; Zr0.01 and Zr0.50 steels show larger and deeper dimples with increased density as Zr content increases.

input_candidates.truth_value: According to thermodynamic calculation (Thermo-Calc, Fig. 7), the equilibrium fraction of ZrC in Zr0.50 steel at 900°C is ~0.6%.
input_candidates.prediction_value: True stress-strain curve of Zr0.50 steel shown as inset in Fig. 4a indicates high true ultimate tensile strength and true strain, but exact values are not provided in text.

input_candidates.truth_value: HDI strengthening contribution in Zr0.50 is estimated to be ~300 MPa (based on Hall-Petch and back-stress models). Precipitation strengthening from ZrC is calculated as ~108 MPa.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Oxygen, nitrogen and hydrogen contents controlled during steelmaking: average O 12–89 ppm, N ~18 ppm, H ~2 ppm.

input_candidates.truth_value: Critical twinning stress for the FCC matrix in Zr0.50 is calculated to be ~1.14 GPa (using SFE of ~55 mJ/m^2), which is lower than the true ultimate tensile strength, enabling deformation twinning.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fig. 3a table indicates Zr0.50 fine grain area fraction of 34%, while abstract and results text state ~35%.

input_candidates.truth_value: Deformation mechanisms in Zr0.50 involve dislocation slip, slip bands, nanotwinning, and HDI hardening; the synergistic effect of HDI hardening and twinning enhances strain hardening and ductility.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Grain size distribution data shown in Fig. 3b–d indicates bimodal distribution in Zr0.50 steel (peaks near 1 μm and 5 μm) versus unimodal distributions in Zr0 (~12 μm) and Zr0.01 (~7 μm).

input_candidates.truth_value: The quasi-in-situ EBSD+μ-DIC analysis (Fig. 5) confirms strain partitioning between soft CG and hard FG zones, with GNDs accumulating at hetero-interfaces, contributing to HDI strengthening.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Stacking fault energy of FCC matrix in Zr0.50 estimated to be ~55 mJ/m^2 from supplementary text S1.
